my_thoughts: The redefinition works: prerequisite = unity + consciousness (not going against desires), plurality of self (many 'I's, not one). The student proposed solution (seeking dualistic protocol), which shows they understand: will = go against desires (dualistic protocol). They'll likely offer example next.
accumulated_insight: T2: Student proposed solution ('Then in order to develop will one must go against desires?')—seeking dualistic protocol via proposes solution and seeks instruction. They understand: will = go against desires (dualistic protocol). The none mechanism redefines: prerequisite = unity + consciousness (not going against desires), plurality of self (many 'I's, not one). Pattern: definition challenge → relativity/definition → protocol question → redefinition → [next: example question]. They're grasping: will = unity + consciousness (not dualistic), self = plural (redefinition).
visible_response:
First of all you must become one.
You are many and you have hundreds of 'I's and
hundreds of wills.
If you want to develop an independent will you must become one
and conscious.
Will depends on unity and consciousness.
